channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: GISEC GLOBAL
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description:
This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots.
Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
item.description: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
item.description: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks steal Microsoft 365 tokens in seconds using fake prompts and OAuth flows. Learn how these MFA bypass tactics work and how to defend against them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description:
The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API.
"In this camp...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.
item.description: IBM and Red Hat assign 20,000 engineers to the new Project Lightwell service as Anthropic's Mythos findings ignite debate over how to secure the open-source software supply chain....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug that removed Copilot buttons in Outlook
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the Copilot Chat or Copilot buttons in Classic Outlook to disappear for Windows users with the Copilot Chat (Basic) license. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
item.description: Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across ente...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA: Microsoft SharePoint RCE flaw now actively exploited
item.description: CISA warned on Wednesday that attackers have begun exploiting a high-severity Microsoft SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability patched in May. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
item.description: Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent.
Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a large...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker extradited to the United States
item.description: A dual United States and Estonian citizen has been extradited to the U.S. to face charges alleging he was a member of the Scattered Spider hacking collective. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
item.description: The recently discovered financially-motivated FortiBleed campaign has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, indicating that the verified, stolen credentials were intended for follow-o...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
item.description: Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC) repositories...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, cit...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Medtronic notifies customers impacted by ShinyHunters data breach
item.description: Healthcare device firm Medtronic is notifying affected customers about a data breach that exposed their personal data to an unauthorized third party. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiBleed credential-theft campaign linked to Lynx ransomware
item.description: The massive FortiBleed credential theft campaign has been linked to the INC and Lynx ransomware operations, suggesting the stolen Fortinet credentials were intended to fuel future network intrusions. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kubota says hackers had month-long access to network systems
item.description: Kubota North America Corporation disclosed that hackers had access to some of its network systems for more than a month earlier this year. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
item.description: Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New ChocoPoC malware targets researchers via trojanized PoC exploits
item.description: Multiple weaponized proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits on GitHub were found delivering a Python-based remote access trojan (RAT) named ChocoPoC that can execute commands and steal sensitive data in a cam...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
item.description: Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
item.description: Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the compone...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
item.description: A teenager accused of belonging to the hacking group Scattered Spider has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud, the U.S. Department of Justice...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
item.description:
Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT.
Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language" cam...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DHS confirms hackers breached HSIN info-sharing platform
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is investigating a cyberattack that compromised the Homeland Security Information Network (HSIN), a sensitive information-sharing platform used by federal, state, l...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs.
The activity h...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Webinar: Why traditional email security is no longer enough
item.description: Modern phishing, business email compromise, and account takeover attacks increasingly exploit trusted identities and legitimate business workflows, making them harder for traditional email defenses to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers target Microsoft 365 accounts with 81 million login attempts
item.description: An aggressive password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments generated more than 81 million login attempts over a two-week period. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
